behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: risk is at module handoff (ingest/parsing -> correlation), not just one function. REAL-INFRA: no explicit OS/network/subprocess requirement. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: no behavior text requiring real external contract/side-effect round-trip, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real ingest + correlation modules together on mixed-service JSON fixtures and assert all records sharing a trace ID end up in the same correlated request group.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: ordering correctness depends on interaction between correlation/timeline components with cross-service timestamps. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: no explicit external contract or round-trip side effect in text, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Feed multi-service correlated entries with controlled timestamp offsets (including +/-500ms skew) through the real timeline assembly path and assert final ordering matches skew-adjusted expectations.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can appear when percentile/baseline computation output is handed to reporting/flagging logic. REAL-INFRA: no explicit requirement for real network/OS/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: text specifies deterministic thresholding, not external contract verification, so System is disqualified; no model output dependency, so Agentic is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Execute metrics aggregation + anomaly reporting modules on synthetic latency distributions where p99 crosses and does not cross 3x baseline, and assert report flags only the true spike case.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is parser + pipeline control-flow + reporting state propagation. REAL-INFRA: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; in-process malformed inputs can reproduce failure. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: no explicit external response contract/round-trip effect requirement, so System is disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real parse-and-process pipeline on mixed valid/invalid JSON records; assert processing completes, valid records continue, and skipped-entry count equals malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: throughput/backpressure and drop behavior depend on runtime scheduling, timing, and actual I/O/process behavior; mocks can hide these failures (MOCK-HIDE). SYSTEM TRIAD trigger: explicit requirement to prove externally visible side effect (no dropped entries, no lag) under real burst conditions via round-trip counts/latency. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because behavior text contains no AI/ML decision dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run the built tool in a realistic process setup, generate a sustained 10,000 events/sec burst, then verify by end-to-end readback/metrics that ingested count equals emitted count and processing lag remains within defined non-falling-behind bounds.
